description:
Goal: define the exact Compiler2-owned native handoff that sits above `BackendProgram(root)` and below the shared native codegen driver. Signal: the handoff shape, invariants, and artifact boundary are explicit in code/tests/docs, and they cover only the facts native codegen truly consumes.

Change:
- identify the minimal native-consumption surface currently read from `Module`, `ModulePlan`, `PlannedProgram`, and `AbiFacts`
- define the corresponding Compiler2-owned handoff data model and contract types
- add contract tests/documentation for the one-way ladder and the forbidden old-world dependencies

Why:
- native cannot honestly consume structured `BackendProgram` directly; the next lowering stage must be deliberate and minimal instead of quietly rebuilding planner state

Checks:
- the handoff contract is grounded in current codegen reads, not guesses
- tests/docs state which old facts disappear and which closed facts remain required
title: Compiler2 native backends: publish the codegen-ready handoff contract
